title: Psmith in the City

quoteFirst: At the appointed hour she was waiting near the entrance of Regent’s Park which Jasper had mentioned. Not long ago there had fallen a light shower, but the sky was clear again. At
quoteLast: she still waited, and had begun to fear that the passing rain might have led Jasper to think she would not come. Another five minutes, and from a hansom that rattled hither at full speed, the familiar figure alighted.
title: New Grub Street
author: Gissing, George, 1857-1903

quoteFirst: 'I'm glad you agree with me. It is now
quoteLast: ; do you think the American family would be taking a siesta?'

quoteFirst: At that same time, and while Her Majesty slept at
quoteLast: at Windsor, the Executive of the Sons of England and Lodge Commercial were in meeting at Shaftesbury Hall, Toronto. Precisely at 10.55 p.m., on Saturday 19th, they sang the National Anthem, commenced that same moment on Sunday afternoon in Levuka, and which for the next 17¼ hours was to be coming steadily nearer with the sun until it was over Toronto at 4 p.m. on Sunday afternoon, when the Sons of England in Toronto again joined in the loyal strain as it pushed by them onwards toward the West.
title:
A Sketch of how "The Diamond Anthem" was Sung around the World
The 60th Anniversary of the Accession Day of Her Majesty Queen Victoria
author: Cumberland, Barlow, 1846-1913
